message:
feat(btree): add UNIQUE index enforcement and fix record comparison semantics

Implement index_insert_unique() for B-tree cursors to enforce UNIQUE
constraints at the storage layer. The method parses record keys, extracts
the first n_unique_cols fields, and checks adjacent cells at the insertion
point for prefix collisions. Multiple NULLs are permitted per SQLite
semantics. The trait default falls back to non-unique insert for mock
cursors.

Additional fixes:

- cursor.rs: Replace raw byte-slice comparison in binary_search_table
  and binary_search_index with record-aware comparison using
  parse_record(). When both keys parse as typed records, compare field
  values via partial_cmp; fall back to byte comparison on parse failure.
  Fix search direction in binary_search_index (was reversed).

- cell.rs: Tighten cell boundary validation to check against
  usable_size (not just page.len()) for both payload bytes and overflow
  pointer offsets. Update error messages and test to use small usable
  size to trigger the bounds check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
